benchmark_report.title: SAVAGE COMMAND BENCHMARKER: PhD-Level Roasting with Statistical Evidence

key_findings: Average execution time: 14.38s (users will check their phones)
Average failure rate: 31.3% (worse odds than a coin flip)
Total cost for 5 commands: $1.99 (more expensive than a coffee for broken tools)
executive_summary.bottom_line: These commands have the performance characteristics of enterprise Java running on a potato

execution_times.savage_commentary: 14.38 seconds average? That's enough time to brew coffee, which users will need to stay awake waiting for these commands.

token_consumption.savage_commentary: 13,246 tokens on average. That's like writing a short story just to ask what time it is.

failure_rates.savage_commentary: 31.3% average failure rate. Even Russian roulette has better odds.

ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md.savage_analysis: This command takes 25.68 seconds to execute with a 46.7% failure rate. That's not 'intelligent thinking', that's digital procrastination. You could order takeout faster than this command completes, and the food would probably be more reliable.

evidence.cost_efficiency: $0.76 per execution - more expensive than most mobile app purchases
evidence.user_experience: Users will abandon this faster than a sinking ship

git-backup-sync.md.savage_analysis: 16.85 seconds to sync git repos with a 40.7% failure rate? This isn't 'intelligent synchronization', it's digital Russian roulette. Your commits have better odds of surviving a hard drive crash than this command succeeding.

evidence.statistical_significance: High failure rate (40.7%) makes this unreliable for production

adhd-task-breakdown.md.savage_analysis: 11.70 seconds to break down a task? By the time this finishes, someone with ADHD has already started three other projects. The irony is palpable.

evidence.statistical_significance: Moderate variance but still exceeds user patience threshold
evidence.target_audience_mismatch: Slow performance contradicts ADHD optimization claims
evidence.failure_rate: 25.9% - too unreliable for users who need consistency

java-rapid-implementation.md.savage_analysis: 8.99 seconds for 'rapid' implementation? The only thing rapid here is how quickly developers will switch to manual coding. At least it's consistently unreliable with that 21.5% failure rate.

evidence.complexity_justification: Moderate complexity for basic functionality indicates over-engineering

safe-code-beautifier.md.savage_analysis: 8.68 seconds to beautify code with a 21.6% failure rate. It's like a plastic surgeon with Parkinson's - the intent is good, but you might not like the results.

evidence.statistical_significance: Lowest variance but still exceeds acceptable response times
evidence.relative_performance: Best of a bad bunch - damning with faint praise
evidence.reliability_concern: 21.6% failure rate for 'safe' operations is ironic

hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: Commands perform within acceptable user experience parameters (< 3s, < 5% failure rate)
hypothesis_testing.test_result: REJECT NULL HYPOTHESIS with extreme prejudice
hypothesis_testing.effect_size: Large (Cohen's d > 0.8) - practically significant performance degradation

complexity_vs_performance.interpretation: Strong positive correlation between complexity score and execution time
complexity_vs_performance.savage_insight: More complex commands are predictably slower, like watching paint dry in slow motion

tokens_vs_failure_rate.interpretation: Higher token consumption correlates with higher failure rates
tokens_vs_failure_rate.savage_insight: The more these commands talk, the more likely they are to fail - like politicians

regression_analysis.interpretation: 88% of execution time variance explained by complexity score
regression_analysis.savage_conclusion: Complexity is killing performance with mathematical precision

immediate_actions.justification: 46.7% failure rate is unacceptable for any production system
immediate_actions.expected_impact: Prevent user frustration and wasted tokens

immediate_actions.justification: Strong correlation (r=0.94) between complexity and poor performance
60% of commands exceed user patience threshold
immediate_actions.expected_impact: Improve user experience through forced optimization

architectural_improvements.recommendation: Implement lazy loading for thinking patterns

architectural_improvements.rationale: Eliminate redundant processing for similar requests

architectural_improvements.recommendation: Simplify command logic following CLAUDE.md complexity rules
architectural_improvements.rationale: Reduce complexity scores to <5 per command

roi_analysis.savage_roi_conclusion: Current commands cost more than they're worth. You'd get better ROI investing in lottery tickets.

final_verdict.savage_summary: This command suite has the performance characteristics of a dial-up modem trying to stream 4K video while running Windows Vista on a calculator. The statistical evidence is overwhelming: these commands are slower than government bureaucracy, less reliable than weather forecasts, and more expensive than artisanal coffee. The only thing 'intelligent' about them is how they've managed to fail so consistently.
final_verdict.bottom_line: COMPLETE OVERHAUL REQUIRED. Current state is scientifically proven to be user-hostile.

outlier_detection.ultrathink_hybrid_mcp: Statistical outlier in all performance metrics
